---
subject: "Best fiction of 2025"
from: "Marginal REVOLUTION <donotreply@wordpress.com>"
to: ""
date: 2025-11-16 08:07:42
labels: ["CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "INBOX", "Tyler Cowen", "UNREAD"]
label_ids: ["CATEGORY_PERSONAL", "INBOX", "Label_2466199532210586699", "UNREAD"]
---
Solvej Balle, [On the Calculation of Volume I, Volume II](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=929223e82ae123d444d437bce868bb3747b833c2891343bc91f60c3c5dfbe659&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91863&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=00c3868fd56bc2c119d45392f5735034&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYW1hem9uLmNvbS9DYWxjdWxhdGlvbi1Cb29rLUlJLVNvbHZlai1CYWxsZS9kcC8wODExMjM3MjczL3JlZj10bW1fcGFwX3N3YXRjaF8w&email_id=4cf81700e0b5a4a512f1f074eccafce2). Volume III is due out in English late this year I have read it already in German. A very strong series, reading ahead in German is a good demonstration of how much I like them.
Suat Dervis, [The Prisoner of Ankara](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=8bb9238b350b130ebf9866fec8034e546ceb84c9c37f7128996a0fde3db8ec52&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91863&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=7d4b57e25afe5109c2f854de01380460&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=4cf81700e0b5a4a512f1f074eccafce2). A Turkish novel from mid-century, in English for the first time.
Emmanuel Carrere, [V13: Chronicle of a Trial](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=98b0f8bb099bee0bdb8575ccd5d16e70c5a0e35ac0265ecc82fbe2a3b26704c2&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91863&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=223c16313a9d8d1832b6e1d524bff021&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=4cf81700e0b5a4a512f1f074eccafce2). Non-fiction but it is more likely reading fiction, it just happens to be true (supposedly).
Alain Mabanckou, [Dealing with the Dead](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=00467df523d645fa9aae8ba00b41654827c1152f0043b0b97f5b125206d4b14a&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91863&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=4ef319306ded0047303e9f621b3bde73&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=4cf81700e0b5a4a512f1f074eccafce2). Most African fiction does not connect with me, and there is a tendency for the reviews to be untrustworthy. This “cemetery memoir,” from the Congo (via UCLA), held my interest throughout.
Kiran Desai, [The Loneliness of Sonia and Sunny](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=c4fe0020af103f856f92236cacfd63a0854a11e3ab8c045f5f3b05abb37584be&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91863&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=d4b64342c6688185fe0f8ad6d8337460&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9tYXJnaW5hbHJldm9sdXRpb24uY29tL21hcmdpbmFscmV2b2x1dGlvbi8yMDI1LzEwL3RoZS1sb25lbGluZXNzLW9mLXNvbmlhLWFuZC1zdW5ueS1ub3ctZmluaXNoZWQuaHRtbA=&email_id=4cf81700e0b5a4a512f1f074eccafce2).
[Eça de Queiros](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=1b7c52a313578bcca1bd6cd2b35e91ac54e7e49aa39a6d810b1a949f083350c8&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91863&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=fb7c3f37df1ab0a86e9f92493e979a1b&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_url=aHR0cHM6Ly9wdC53aWtpcGVkaWEub3JnL3dpa2kvRSVDMyVBN2FfZGVfUXVlaXJveg=&email_id=4cf81700e0b5a4a512f1f074eccafce2), [Adam and Eve in Paradise](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=9d10d74ef8cb5963df06971e5162fd7f2d1afc2c33a0931d90afb8f5595f5161&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91863&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=4b786c3cec1aee582e60b24d7d2cd816&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=4cf81700e0b5a4a512f1f074eccafce2). Originally from the 19th century, but translated into English only this year. A 60 pp. novella about exactly what the title indicates, noting that matters are not as simple as the first telling of that story might have suggested.
[The Poems of Seamus Heaney](http://marginalrevolution.com?action=user_content_redirect&uuid=8c0bedf72644193eb127f963bbb56b4fe9e1a6c9c4e016d5c507b6e9672bfef8&blog_id=42693868&post_id=91863&user_id=262258391&subs_id=225460721&signature=b00d011ecdb64003cbddbd16da23ef22&email_name=new-post&user_email=ishan.mail@gmail.com&encoded_&email_id=4cf81700e0b5a4a512f1f074eccafce2). Not yet received, but obviously this is a winner.
Overall, the Balle, Desai, and Heaney make for very strong entries, so this was a good year for fiction.
